title: Quiz: why validate on the backend if the frontend already does?
prompt_md: Your React app already validates the form before submitting. Why must the backend validate the same input again?
options: It doesn't need to — duplicating validation is wasted effort if the frontend is correct
Because the client can be bypassed or tampered with (curl, Postman, modified JS, malicious users), so the server is the only trustworthy boundary and must enforce the rules itself
Only to show nicer error messages than the frontend can
Because backend validation makes the database queries run faster
explanation_md: **Never trust the client.** Frontend validation is purely a UX nicety — anyone can skip your JS and hit the API directly with **curl/Postman**, replay or tamper with requests, or send malformed/malicious payloads. The server is the only **trust boundary** you actually control, so it must independently validate types, ranges, and authorization (and guard against injection). Frame it as *defense in depth*: client validation improves experience, server validation enforces correctness and security.
